title: rubus caesius subsp. pusillus
intro:
Rubus caesius subsp. pusillus, also known as the dwarf blackberry or little blackberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, characterized by its small, dark purple fruits and thorny stems.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve pain.

The health benefits of Rubus caesius subsp. pusillus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. 

The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its health benefits.
